response: solution The primary error in the erroneous code is failing to handle duplicate elements correctly when they exceed half the array length, causing adjacent equal values. solution A robust approach involves sorting the array and then placing elements from the sorted middle to the end in odd indices and the rest in even indices. solution This strategy ensures that the largest elements are separated by smaller ones, minimizing the chance of adjacent duplicates. solution For the follow-up question, an O(n) solution without full sorting can be achieved using the QuickSelect algorithm to find the median. solution Once the median is found, we can partition the array into elements less than, equal to, and greater than the median in linear time. solution This partitioning allows us to construct the wiggle pattern by interleaving the two halves without needing a full sort. solution The method works equally well for negative integers because the comparison logic remains consistent regardless of the sign. solution The final implementation guarantees that adjacent elements are not equal whenever a valid permutation exists.
